findings.content: allowed-tools: Bash(git:*) Bash(printenv CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT) Bash(printenv __CFBundleIdentifier) Bash(printenv VSCODE_PID) Bash(mkdir:*) Bash(ls:*) Bash(wc:*) Bash(cd:*) Read Write Edit Grep Glob
findings.explanation: Frontmatter declares explicit, narrowly scoped permissions. Previous version used wildcard Bash(printenv:*) and Bash(env:*) — hardened to allowlist only the three IDE-detection environment variables actually consumed (CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT, __CFBundleIdentifier, VSCODE_PID). Bash(git:*) is required to fetch the PR branch via 'git fetch origin pull/<N>/head:pr-<N>' and diff it against the base branch. No exfiltration vector.

findings.content: Data flow: git fetch pull/<N>/head + diff vs base -> mental analysis -> markdown report in <ide>/Revisiones PRs/
findings.explanation: All flows are local. Fetches the PR branch via git, diffs it against the base, reads source files in the diff, writes a structured markdown report under .antigravity/.cursor/.vscode/docs. No network destinations beyond git fetch, no MCP calls, no credential access.

notes: Drupal 11 pull-request code review skill following the Codex methodology (18 production-tested rules). Sibling skill of codex-diff-develop with identical references and structure — only the target differs (PR branch via 'git fetch origin pull/<N>/head' instead of 'origin/develop'). Pure local-analysis tool: no bundled scripts, no MCP references, no credential file access. Frontmatter hardened on 2026-04-07 to replace wildcard env access with an explicit three-variable allowlist for IDE detection. Reputation layer scored 50 (neutral) because the skill is first-party in this same repository; cannot independently verify.
